ddls:
CREATE TABLE "medical_terms" (
  "term_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each medical term */,
  "term_uri" TEXT /* Unique URI for the medical term */,
  "label" TEXT /* Label or name of the medical term */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Detailed comment or description of the medical term */,
  "enumeration_type_uri" TEXT /* URI of the enumeration type associated with the term */,
  "supersedes_id" INTEGER /* ID of the term this term supersedes */,
  "superseded_by_id" INTEGER /* ID of the term that supersedes this term */,
  "part_of_id" INTEGER /* ID of the part of the healthcare domain this term belongs to */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* ID of the status of the medical term */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the medical term */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the medical term was created */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the medical term */,
  "last_updated_date" TEXT /* Date when the medical term was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("term_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medical_terms_superseded_by_id FOREIGN KEY ("superseded_by_id") REFERENCES medical_terms ("term_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medical_terms_part_of_id FOREIGN KEY ("part_of_id") REFERENCES healthcare_domains ("domain_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medical_terms_enumeration_type_uri FOREIGN KEY ("enumeration_type_uri") REFERENCES enumeration_types ("type_uri"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medical_terms_supersedes_id FOREIGN KEY ("supersedes_id") REFERENCES medical_terms ("term_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "enumeration_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each enumeration type */,
  "type_uri" TEXT /* Unique URI for the enumeration type */,
  "type_label" TEXT /* Label or name of the enumeration type */,
  "type_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the enumeration type */,
  "parent_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent enumeration type */,
  "description_long" TEXT /* Detailed description of the enumeration type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "healthcare_domains" (
  "domain_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each healthcare domain */,
  "domain_uri" TEXT /* Unique URI for the healthcare domain */,
  "domain_label" TEXT /* Label or name of the healthcare domain */,
  "domain_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the healthcare domain */,
  "parent_domain_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent healthcare domain */,
  "description_long" TEXT /* Detailed description of the healthcare domain */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("domain_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "term_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "from_term_id" INTEGER /* ID of the term that is the source of the relationship */,
  "to_term_id" INTEGER /* ID of the term that is the target of the relationship */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., supersedes, supersededBy) */,
  "relationship_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the relationship between medical terms */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_term_relationships_to_term_id FOREIGN KEY ("to_term_id") REFERENCES medical_terms ("term_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_term_relationships_from_term_id FOREIGN KEY ("from_term_id") REFERENCES medical_terms ("term_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., clinician, researcher, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or organization the user belongs to */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "term_id" INTEGER /* ID of the medical term being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the term */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the term was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address from which the medical term was accessed */,
  "access_user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the browser or application used to access the medical term */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_term_id FOREIGN KEY ("term_id") REFERENCES medical_terms ("term_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "term_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_label" TEXT /* Label or name of the category */,
  "category_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the category */,
  "category_description_long" TEXT /* Detailed description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "term_usages" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage event */,
  "term_id" INTEGER /* ID of the medical term being used */,
  "context_id" INTEGER /* ID of the context in which the term is used */,
  "usage_date" TEXT /* Date when the term was used */,
  "usage_description" TEXT /* Description of the context in which the term was used */,
  "usage_context" TEXT /* Detailed context in which the medical term was used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_term_usages_term_id FOREIGN KEY ("term_id") REFERENCES medical_terms ("term_id")
)
